brand_summary: Cape Coast Coffee is a premium single-origin coffee brand selling direct-to-consumer, currently held back by a sluggish WooCommerce store that's difficult to manage and underperforming in search. The opportunity is clear: migrate to Shopify for a faster, cleaner buying experience, then layer in proper e-commerce SEO to capture the growing organic demand for specialty coffee in South Africa.

realities.title: The Platform Problem
realities.text: The current WooCommerce store is slow, clunky, and a pain to manage day-to-day. It's costing Cape Coast Coffee sales — both through poor page speed killing conversions and through the time Sarah's team spends wrestling with the backend instead of growing the brand. A move to Shopify fixes this at the foundation level.

realities.title: The Organic Gap
realities.text: Premium single-origin coffee is a category where consumers actively search for information — origin stories, brewing guides, tasting notes. Right now, Cape Coast Coffee isn't showing up for those queries. There's a significant organic traffic opportunity being left on the table, and the current site's technical limitations make it nearly impossible to rank well.

realities.title: The Timing
realities.text: Festive season is the biggest DTC gifting window of the year, and coffee is a natural fit. Launching a new store and building organic momentum before Q4 means Cape Coast Coffee can capitalise on seasonal demand rather than scrambling to catch up. The timeline is tight but achievable if we move decisively.

plan.title: Audit & Migration Planning
items: Full WooCommerce site audit — products, customers, orders, integrations, URL structure
SEO/AEO audit to baseline current organic performance and identify quick wins

plan.title: Store Build & Data Migration
items: Shopify theme build with brand customisation, mobile-first responsive design, and full product migration from WooCommerce
301 redirect mapping to preserve all existing SEO equity during the migration

plan.title: Optimisation & Configuration
items: Payment gateway setup (PayFast/Yoco), shipping configuration, checkout flow optimisation with trust signals and upsells
On-page SEO foundations — metadata, schema markup, heading structure, alt tags across all migrated content

plan.title: QA, Launch & Handover
items: Cross-browser and device testing, checkout simulation, page speed optimisation targeting 80+ PageSpeed Insights score
DNS cutover, go-live, 48-hour post-launch monitoring, and full store handover to the Cape Coast Coffee team

plan.title: Organic Growth Engine
items: Ongoing E-Commerce SEO Lite — 20 target keywords, product and collection page optimisation, 2 blogs/month, backlink building
AEO layer with FAQ schemas, featured snippet targeting, and AI-friendly product descriptions to show up in ChatGPT and Google AI Overviews

cost.line_1_desc: Full WooCommerce migration, Shopify theme build, product/customer/order migration, 301 redirects, payment & shipping config, QA & launch

deliverables.title: Migration & Store
items: Full WooCommerce → Shopify migration (products, customers, orders)
Custom-branded Shopify theme build with all core pages
301 redirect implementation for SEO equity preservation
Payment gateway and shipping/logistics configuration

deliverables.title: Performance & QA
items: Page speed optimisation (80+ PageSpeed Insights)
Cross-browser and device QA
Checkout simulation and go-live smoke test
Branded email notification templates

deliverables.title: SEO Foundations
items: SEO/AEO audit with prioritised action plan
20 target keywords researched and mapped
Product schema markup (10 products/month)
Metadata and alt-tag optimisation across store

deliverables.title: Ongoing SEO & Content
items: 2 SEO blog posts per month (800–1,200 words)
5–10 quality backlinks per month
3 FAQ schemas and 3 featured snippet targets per month
5 AI-friendly product descriptions per month

timeline: 10–14 weeks from kick-off to organic growth phase. Migration and store build in weeks 1–6, SEO audit and foundations in weeks 4–8 (overlapping), ongoing SEO retainer from launch. Targeting a pre-festive season go-live.
out_of_scope: Brand/logo design, social media management, paid advertising (Google/Meta Ads), copywriting beyond SEO blog posts, custom Shopify app development, photography or product shoots

to_get_started.title: WooCommerce Access
to_get_started.text: Admin access to the current WooCommerce store so we can run the full migration audit — products, customers, orders, plugins, and URL structure.

to_get_started.title: Brand Assets
to_get_started.text: Logo files (SVG/PNG), brand colour codes, typography preferences, and any high-resolution product photography you have available.

to_get_started.title: Product & Content Review
to_get_started.text: Confirmation of which products, collections, and pages should migrate. Flag anything that's outdated or should be retired — this is a clean-slate opportunity.

to_get_started.title: Payment & Shipping Details
to_get_started.text: Preferred payment gateway (PayFast, Yoco, or Peach Payments) and shipping setup — flat rates, courier integration (Bob Go, Pargo), or manual configuration.

to_get_started.title: SEO Inputs
to_get_started.text: Any existing keyword research, Google Analytics/Search Console access, and a list of your top competitors so we can hit the ground running on SEO strategy.

to_get_started.title: Sign-Off & Deposit
to_get_started.text: Signed proposal and 50% deposit on the migration build (R29,000) to lock in the timeline. SEO retainer kicks in from the month of launch.
